A function checks if a number is prime or composite. It uses modulus to find remainders.  
The status is prime if no divisors exist. The status is composite if one exists.  
* * *  
A number must be greater than one to be valid. The status depends on its factors.  
The modulus operator compares values. The status reveals its nature.  
* * *  
Each test divides the number by two. The status confirms its type.  
The function returns a clear status. The status defines its role.
